# Faultsense

> Faultsense is a lightweight (6.5KB gzipped) browser agent that validates feature correctness in production through declarative HTML attribute assertions. Think E2E test assertions, but running against real user sessions.

- Docs: https://www.faultsense.org/docs
- Full reference: [llms-full.txt](./llms-full.txt)
- Examples: https://www.faultsense.org/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/Fault-Sense/faultsense-agent

## How It Works

Add `fs-*` HTML attributes to the element a user interacts with. The attributes declare: what event triggers the assertion, what DOM outcome is expected, and how to verify it. The agent monitors real user sessions and reports pass/fail results to a collector.

## Attribute Reference

### Required

- `fs-assert="<key>"` — Assertion key. Use `/` to group: `"checkout/add-to-cart"`, `"profile/media/upload-photo"`
- `fs-trigger="<value>"` — One of: `click`, `dblclick`, `change`, `blur`, `submit`, `mount`, `unmount`, `load`, `error`, `invariant`

### Assertion Types (at least 1 required)

Value is a CSS selector, optionally with inline modifiers in brackets:

- `fs-assert-added="<selector>"` — Element added to DOM
- `fs-assert-removed="<selector>"` — Element removed from DOM
- `fs-assert-updated="<selector>"` — Element or subtree mutated
- `fs-assert-visible="<selector>"` — Element exists and visible
- `fs-assert-hidden="<selector>"` — Element exists but hidden
- `fs-assert-loaded="<selector>"` — Media element finished loading

Conditional — append a condition key to the type name:
- `fs-assert-{type}-{condition-key}="<selector>"` — e.g., `fs-assert-added-success=".dashboard"`, `fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg"`
- Condition keys are freeform lowercase alphanumeric strings with hyphens (e.g., `success`, `error`, `empty`, `rate-limited`).
- Multiple condition keys on the same element and type form a sibling group. First to resolve wins, others dismissed.
- `fs-assert-grouped=""` — Groups all conditionals on the element as siblings regardless of type. Use for mutually exclusive outcomes that need different assertion types (e.g., `removed-success` + `added-error`).

### Inline Modifiers (in the value)

Chained CSS-like brackets after the selector:

- `[text-matches=pattern]` — Text content regex/string match
- `[classlist=active:true,hidden:false]` — Class presence check
- `[attr=value]` — Any other key is an attribute check

Example: `fs-assert-updated='#count[text-matches=\d+]'`

### Element-Level Attributes

- `fs-assert-timeout="<ms>"` — Override default timeout (default: 1000ms)
- `fs-assert-mpa="true"` — Persist assertion across page navigation
- `fs-assert-grouped=""` — Group all conditionals on element as siblings across types

### Assertion Key Convention

Use `/` separators to group assertions hierarchically. Nest as deep as needed:

```
fs-assert="checkout/add-to-cart"
fs-assert="checkout/submit-order"
fs-assert="profile/media/upload-photo"
fs-assert="auth/login"
```

Keys must be stable across releases. Human-readable labels are configured on the collector side, not in the HTML.

## Placement Rule

Attributes go on the element that receives the user event. Only the exact `event.target` is processed.

## Examples

### Button click updates DOM element

```html
<button
  fs-assert="counter/increment"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated='#count[text-matches=Count: [1-9]\d*]'>
  Increment
</button>
<span id="count">Count: 0</span>
```

### Form with conditional assertions

```html
<form
  fs-assert="contact/submit-form"
  fs-trigger="submit"
  fs-assert-added-success=".success-msg"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg"
  fs-assert-timeout="2000">
  <input name="email" type="email" />
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

### Cross-type grouped conditionals

```html
<button
  fs-assert="todos/remove-item"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-grouped=""
  fs-assert-removed-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg"
  fs-assert-timeout="5000">
  Delete
</button>
```

### Initial state validation on page load

```html
<div
  fs-assert="todo/initial-state"
  fs-trigger="mount"
  fs-assert-visible=".todos-empty">
  <!-- app content -->
</div>
```

### Invariant assertion (continuous monitoring)

```html
<nav id="main-nav"
  fs-assert="layout/nav-visible"
  fs-trigger="invariant"
  fs-assert-visible="#main-nav">
</nav>
```

Invariants stay pending and produce no collector traffic while the condition holds. Only failures (violations) and recoveries are reported. On page unload, pending invariants are auto-passed as the "all clear" signal.

### OOB assertion (side-effect verification)

```html
<!-- Primary assertion on the trigger element -->
<button fs-assert="todos/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-added-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".add-error">Add</button>

<!-- OOB: verify count updated when add succeeds -->
<div id="todo-count"
  fs-assert="todos/count-updated"
  fs-assert-oob-visible="todos/add-item"
  fs-assert-visible="[text-matches=\d+/\d+ remaining]">
</div>
```

OOB assertions fire when a referenced parent assertion passes. Use for side-effect elements (counts, toasts, totals) without prop drilling. Use state types (`visible`, `hidden`, `added`, `removed`) — not event types (`updated`, `loaded`).

### Multi-check conditional outcome (OOB pattern)

```html
<!-- Delete: conditional success/error -->
<button fs-assert="todos/remove-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-grouped=""
  fs-assert-removed-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg">Delete</button>

<!-- OOB: verify toast shown on successful delete -->
<div class="toast-container"
  fs-assert="todos/delete-toast"
  fs-assert-oob-visible="todos/remove-item"
  fs-assert-visible=".success-toast">
</div>
```

To verify multiple things on one conditional outcome, use OOB on secondary elements. The conditional handles the primary check; OOB handles secondary checks triggered by the conditional's success.
